examples.title: Basic OS Boot Process
examples.description: Demonstrates what happens when computer starts
examples.code:
PROGRAM Bootstrap
BEGIN
  POWER_ON:
    Initialize CPU registers
    Load BIOS from ROM
    
  POST (Power-On Self-Test):
    Check hardware components
    Display system configuration
    
  BOOT_LOADER:
    Read boot sector from storage device
    Load OS kernel into memory
    
  KERNEL_INITIALIZATION:
    Setup memory management
    Initialize device drivers
    Start system processes
    
  USER_SPACE:
    Launch login/shell process
    Display login prompt
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows bootstrap sequence from power-on through kernel loading to user login. Bootstrap is crucial as it transitions computer from hardware-only state to operating system controlled state.

examples.title: System Call Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how user programs request OS services
examples.code:
import os
import sys

# Simulating system calls
class SystemCall:
    def __init__(self):
        self.process_table = []
        self.memory = [0] * 1000
        
    def fork_process(self):
        """Simulates fork() system call - creates new process"""
        new_pid = len(self.process_table) + 1
        self.process_table.append({
            'pid': new_pid,
            'state': 'RUNNING',
            'memory': 100  # Allocate 100 units
        })
        return new_pid
    
    def allocate_memory(self, size):
        """Simulates brk/sbrk system call - changes data segment size"""
        for i in range(1000):
            if self.memory[i] == 0:
                allocated = min(size, 1000 - i)
                for j in range(i, i + allocated):
                    self.memory[j] = 1
                return i, allocated
        return None, 0
    
    def read_file(self, filename):
        """Simulates read() system call for file I/O"""
        # OS checks permissions, opens file, reads data
        print(f"OS: Reading file '{filename}' on behalf of user process")
        return "file contents"
    
    def exit_process(self, status):
        """Simulates exit() system call - terminates process"""
        print(f"OS: Process terminated with status {status}")
        self.process_table.clear()

# Usage demonstration
os = SystemCall()
pid = os.fork_process()
print(f"Created process with PID: {pid}")
base, size = os.allocate_memory(50)
print(f"Allocated memory at {base}, size {size}")
os.read_file("example.txt")
os.exit_process(0)
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates system calls that user programs use to request OS services. Real system calls involve mode switching from user to kernel mode for security.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for process creation, O(n) for memory allocation
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for process table and memory arrays

examples.title: CPU Scheduling Simulation (Round Robin)
examples.description: Demonstrates OS process management and CPU allocation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM RoundRobinScheduler
INPUT: Process queue Q, Time quantum q
VARIABLES: Current time t, Running process P

BEGIN
  INITIALIZE:
    t = 0
    FOR each process in ready_queue:
        process.remaining_time = process.burst_time
        process.state = "READY"
  
  WHILE ready_queue is not empty OR running_process exists:
    
    IF running_process == NULL THEN
      // OS selects next process from queue
      IF ready_queue not empty THEN
        running_process = DEQUEUE(ready_queue)
        running_process.state = "RUNNING"
      ENDIF
    ENDIF
    
    IF running_process != NULL THEN
      // Execute process for quantum or remaining time
      exec_time = MIN(quantum, running_process.remaining_time)
      
      RUN running_process for exec_time
      t = t + exec_time
      running_process.remaining_time = running_process.remaining_time - exec_time
      
      // Check if process completed
      IF running_process.remaining_time == 0 THEN
        running_process.state = "TERMINATED"
        PRINT "Process", running_process.pid, "completed at time", t
        running_process = NULL
      ELSE
        // Process preempted, goes back to queue
        running_process.state = "READY"
        ENQUEUE(ready_queue, running_process)
        running_process = NULL
      ENDIF
    ENDIF
    
  ENDWHILE
  
  PRINT "All processes completed. Total time:", t
END
examples.explanation: Round Robin is a CPU scheduling algorithm where each process gets equal time slice. This demonstrates OS role as resource allocator deciding which process gets CPU and for how long.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of processes

examples.title: Memory Management Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates OS memory allocation and protection
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates OS memory management functions: allocation (providing memory to processes), tracking (which process owns which memory), and deallocation (freeing memory when process terminates). Shows memory protection as each process gets isolated memory space.
